zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabell Martin於1575年出生時，她的父親William Martin35歲，母親Margaret Collyn35歲。她於1595年9月21日與Thomas Doggett在英國英格蘭肯特古度爾斯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1641年10月14日在英國英格蘭肯特霍斯蒙登享年66歲，埋葬在英國肯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabell Martin于1575年出生时，她的父亲William Martin35岁，母亲Margaret Collyn35岁。她于1595年9月21日与Thomas Doggett在英国英格兰肯特古度爾斯特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1641年10月14日在英国英格兰肯特霍斯蒙登享年66岁，埋葬在英国肯特。

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Isabell Martin was born in 1575, her father, William Martin, was 35 and her mother, Margaret Collyn, was 35. She married Thomas Doggett on 21 September 1595, in Goudhurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 14 October 1641, in Horsmonden, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 66, and was buried in Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabell Martin est née en 1575, son père, William Martin, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Margaret Collyn, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Doggett le 21 septembre 1595, à Goudhurst, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 octobre 1641, à Horsmonden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabell Martin 1575 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Martin 35 und ihre Mutter Margaret Collyn 35. Sie hat am 21. September 1595 in Goudhurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom Thomas Doggett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 14. Oktober 1641 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Horsmonden, Kent, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabell Martin è nata il nel 1575, sua padre, William Martin, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Margaret Collyn, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Doggett il 21 settembre 1595, nel Goudhurst, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 14 ottobre 1641, nel Horsmonden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabell Martinが1575年に生まれた時，父William Martinは35歳，母Margaret Collynは35歳であった。1595年9月21日にイギリスイギリスケントゴウドハルストでThomas Doggettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1641年10月14日にイギリスイギリスケントホールスモンデンで66歳で死去し，イギリスケントにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabell Martin(이)가 1575년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Martin(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Collyn(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1595년 9월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 고우드후르스트에서 Thomas Doggett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1641년 10월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 호르스몽에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabell Martin nasceu em 1575, seu pai, William Martin, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Collyn, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Doggett em 21 de setembro de 1595, em Goudhurst, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 14 de outubro de 1641, em Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabell Martin родилась в 1575 г., её отцу по имени William Martin было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Collyn, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Doggett 21 сентября 1595 г. в местности гоудхурст, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 14 октября 1641 г. в местности хаурсмонден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabell Martin nació en 1575, su papá, William Martin, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Margaret Collyn, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Thomas Doggett el 21 de septiembre de 1595, en Goudhurst, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 14 de octubre de 1641, en Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en Kent, Inglaterra.
